summary:
The patient exhibits significant conjugated hyperbilirubinemia (82% of total bilirubin) along with dramatically elevated ALP and GGT, pointing towards pathological cholestasis. This excludes benign conditions like breast milk jaundice and supports a diagnosis of biliary atresia.  

\boxed{B}

Okay, I need to summarize only what Agent1’s reasoning says about the key clinical points related to this case.

First sentence should highlight why breast milk jaundice isn’t correct – due to abnormal conjugation patterns and enzyme elevations. Second sentence focuses on how those specific labs support diagnosing biliary atresia instead.

I’ll keep both under two full sentences strictly focused on diagnostic logic based solely on laboratory values and their implications.
